metadata.title: Prefect/MLflow/Docker Code Review Cleanup
metadata.description: Fix 15 code quality issues identified in the deep review of PR #400 (28-task Prefect/Docker hardening). Issues fall into five categories: 1. DRY violations (MLflow init, FlowContract, log_flow_completion, try-except) 2. Naming consistency (env vars, @flow decorators, MLflow tags) 3. Error handling (silent failures, sentinel strings, bare except) 4. Missing features (deploy_flow config default, preflight validation, upstream IDs) 5. Type improvements (PostTrainingFlowResult dataclass, return annotations)

verify_commands.command: uv run ruff check src/minivess/orchestration/ tests/unit/orchestration/

task.title: Create orchestration constants module
task.description: All MLflow experiment name strings are hardcoded across 6+ flow files. A typo causes silent cross-flow isolation failure. Create src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py with all experiment names and flow name constants as module-level string constants.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_constants.py: - test_experiment_names_are_strings: all EXPERIMENT_* constants are non-empty strings - test_flow_names_are_lowercase_hyphen: all FLOW_NAME_* constants match pattern [a-z][a-z-]* - test_no_duplicate_values: all experiment names are unique - test_all_expected_constants_exist: check for EXPERIMENT_TRAINING, EXPERIMENT_DATA, EXPERIMENT_EVALUATION, EXPERIMENT_POST_TRAINING, EXPERIMENT_DEPLOYMENT, EXPERIMENT_DASHBOARD, EXPERIMENT_QA, FLOW_NAME_TRAIN, FLOW_NAME_DATA, FLOW_NAME_POST_TRAINING, FLOW_NAME_ANALYSIS, FLOW_NAME_DEPLOY, FLOW_NAME_DASHBOARD, FLOW_NAME_QA
tdd_spec.green_phase: Create src/minivess/orchestration/constants.py: - EXPERIMENT_* string constants for each MLflow experiment - FLOW_NAME_* string constants for each Prefect flow (lowercase-hyphen) - Module docstring explaining the naming convention - Export via __init__.py
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All constants module tests pass - No hardcoded experiment/flow name strings remain in flow files (verified in T-06) - mypy: clean

task.title: Extract open_mlflow_run() helper to eliminate 6-flow duplication
task.description: The 3-liner (set_tracking_uri + set_experiment + start_run) is duplicated across all 6 flows. Create src/minivess/orchestration/mlflow_helpers.py with a context-manager helper that wraps this pattern.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_mlflow_helpers.py: - test_open_mlflow_run_sets_uri: mock mlflow; verify set_tracking_uri called - test_open_mlflow_run_sets_experiment: verify set_experiment called - test_open_mlflow_run_enters_context: verify start_run() called and run_id returned - test_open_mlflow_run_with_tags: verify tags passed through to start_run() - test_open_mlflow_run_exception_propagates: verify exceptions are not swallowed
tdd_spec.green_phase: Create src/minivess/orchestration/mlflow_helpers.py: - open_mlflow_run(tracking_uri, experiment_name, tags) -> contextlib.AbstractContextManager that calls set_tracking_uri, set_experiment, then starts and yields the run - log_completion_safe(flow_name, tracking_uri, run_id) -> None helper that wraps FlowContract.log_flow_completion() with safe exception handling - find_upstream_safely(tracking_uri, experiment_name, upstream_flow) -> dict | None helper that wraps FlowContract.find_upstream_run() returning None on exception
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All helper tests pass - Helpers are importable from minivess.orchestration.mlflow_helpers - mypy: clean with full type annotations

task.title: Deduplicate FlowContract imports — import at module level, reuse instance
task.description: FlowContract is imported inside try-except blocks twice per flow function. Move import to module top-level; create a single FlowContract instance per tracking_uri within each flow call, reuse it for both find_upstream_run() and log_flow_completion() calls.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_contract_dedup.py: - test_flow_contract_import_is_top_level: use ast.parse() + ast.walk() to verify FlowContract import is NOT inside a try-except block in any flow file (checked files: train_flow.py, post_training_flow.py, deploy_flow.py, analysis_flow.py) - test_find_upstream_uses_contract: verify that after T-02 helpers are applied, each flow calls find_upstream_safely() (not raw FlowContract) inside functions
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update all 4 affected flows: - Move FlowContract import to module top-level (from minivess.orchestration.flow_contract) - In each flow function: create one contract instance, call find_upstream_safely() and log_completion_safe() from mlflow_helpers (T-02) - Remove the duplicate try-except import pattern
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - No FlowContract import inside try-except blocks (AST-checked) - Existing flow tests still pass - ruff/mypy: clean

task.title: Replace inline log_flow_completion try-except with log_completion_safe() helper
task.description: The FlowContract.log_flow_completion() call wrapped in try-except is duplicated verbatim in 5 flows. Replace with the log_completion_safe() helper from T-02.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Extend tests/unit/orchestration/test_mlflow_helpers.py: - test_log_completion_safe_calls_contract: mock FlowContract; verify log_flow_completion called - test_log_completion_safe_handles_exception: exception from FlowContract is caught; warning logged - test_log_completion_safe_skips_none_run_id: when run_id is None, no FlowContract call made
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update all 5 flows (train_flow, post_training_flow, analysis_flow, deploy_flow, dashboard_flow): - Replace inline try-except + FlowContract instantiation with: from minivess.orchestration.mlflow_helpers import log_completion_safe log_completion_safe(FLOW_NAME_*, tracking_uri, mlflow_run_id) - Use FLOW_NAME_* constants from T-01 for the flow_name argument
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All 5 flows use log_completion_safe() instead of inline boilerplate - Tests for log_completion_safe pass - No regression in existing flow tests

task.title: Merge adjacent try-except blocks into single per-flow block
task.description: After T-02–T-04, the remaining try-except blocks in flows can be merged or eliminated. Verify that each flow function has at most one infrastructure try-except block after the DRY refactors are applied.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_structure.py: - test_train_flow_single_try_block: use ast.parse() + ast.walk() to count top-level Try nodes in training_flow() body; assert count <= 1 - test_post_training_flow_single_try_block: same for post_training_flow() - test_analysis_flow_single_try_block: same for run_analysis_flow() - test_deploy_flow_single_try_block: same for deploy_flow() NOTE: AST parsing is MANDATORY — no regex (CLAUDE.md Rule #16)
tdd_spec.green_phase: For each flow where T-03/T-04 leaves adjacent try-except blocks: - Merge into a single try-except that handles both upstream discovery and MLflow setup - OR: verify that helpers already eliminated the blocks and the test passes as-is The goal is clean, readable flow bodies without boilerplate repetition.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - AST test: at most 1 top-level try block in each main flow function - All existing flow tests still pass - ruff format: clean (no excessive nesting)

task.title: Standardize all @flow decorator names to lowercase-hyphen style
task.description: Flow decorator names use inconsistent styles (spaces, title-case, lowercase-hyphen). Prefect convention is lowercase-hyphen. Standardize all flows and update deployments.py to use the FLOW_NAME_* constants from T-01.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_names.py: - test_all_flow_names_lowercase_hyphen: use ast.parse() + ast.walk() to find @flow(name=...) decorator calls in all flow files; assert each name matches pattern r'^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$' — NOTE: use str.match() not regex compile Actually: use str.lower() + str.replace(' ', '-') comparison, not regex (Rule #16) Better: check name == name.lower() and ' ' not in name and '_' not in name - test_deployments_keys_match_flow_names: import deployments.py FLOW_DEPLOYMENTS dict; assert all keys are values of FLOW_NAME_* constants from constants.py - test_constants_match_decorators: verify FLOW_NAME_TRAIN == "training-flow" etc.
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update flow files: - post_training_flow.py: @flow(name="Post-Training Pipeline") → @flow(name=FLOW_NAME_POST_TRAINING) - deploy_flow.py: @flow(name="Deploy Pipeline") → @flow(name=FLOW_NAME_DEPLOY) - dashboard_flow.py: @flow(name="minivess-dashboard") → @flow(name=FLOW_NAME_DASHBOARD) - Others: verify and align with constants Update deployments.py: replace string keys with FLOW_NAME_* constants
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All @flow names are lowercase-hyphen and use FLOW_NAME_* constants - deployments.py keys match constants - Tests pass

task.title: Fix flow name vs MLflow tag mismatch — FlowContract.find_upstream_run() was broken
task.description: FlowContract.find_upstream_run() searches MLflow by the flow_name tag that flows write when logging completion. But the tag values (e.g., "analyze") don't match the @flow decorator names (e.g., "analysis-flow"), causing silent discovery failure. After T-06, all names are standardized; now ensure logged tags match constants.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Extend tests/unit/orchestration/test_flow_names.py: - test_logged_flow_name_matches_flow_decorator: for each flow, verify that the flow_name argument passed to log_flow_completion() equals the @flow(name=) value (check via AST walk on the flow function body) Write tests/integration/orchestration/test_flow_chain_discovery.py: - test_training_flow_can_be_discovered_by_analysis: mock MLflow; training_flow logs completion with FLOW_NAME_TRAIN tag; analysis_flow discovery query returns it - test_post_training_can_be_discovered_by_deploy: same for post_training → deploy chain
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update all log_flow_completion() calls to use FLOW_NAME_* constants: - train_flow.py: log_completion_safe(FLOW_NAME_TRAIN, ...) - post_training_flow.py: log_completion_safe(FLOW_NAME_POST_TRAINING, ...) - analysis_flow.py: log_completion_safe(FLOW_NAME_ANALYSIS, ...) - deploy_flow.py: log_completion_safe(FLOW_NAME_DEPLOY, ...) - dashboard_flow.py: log_completion_safe(FLOW_NAME_DASHBOARD, ...) Update FlowContract.find_upstream_run() call sites to use FLOW_NAME_* for the upstream_flow argument.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - Tag logged by each flow matches the constant; integration test passes - FlowContract queries use FLOW_NAME_* constants (no hardcoded strings) - End-to-end chain discovery test passes

task.title: Standardize output directory env vars to _OUTPUT_DIR suffix
task.description: Output directory env vars use inconsistent naming: ANALYSIS_OUTPUT vs POST_TRAINING_OUTPUT_DIR. Standardize all to use _OUTPUT_DIR suffix and update docker-compose.flows.yml and all Dockerfiles accordingly.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_env_var_naming.py: - test_output_dir_env_vars_have_dir_suffix: use ast.parse() + ast.walk() to find os.environ.get("...OUTPUT...") calls in all flow files; assert each name ends with "_OUTPUT_DIR" (use str.endswith(), not regex) - test_docker_compose_uses_dir_suffix: read deployment/docker-compose.flows.yml with yaml.safe_load(); find all env var names containing "OUTPUT"; assert all end with "_DIR"
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update flow files: - analysis_flow.py: ANALYSIS_OUTPUT → ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR - dashboard_flow.py: DASHBOARD_OUTPUT → DASHBOARD_OUTPUT_DIR Update docker-compose.flows.yml: rename env vars in all services that use OUTPUT Update Dockerfile.analyze and Dockerfile.dashboard: update ENV defaults Update any scripts that set these env vars
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - No env vars ending in _OUTPUT (without _DIR) in flow code or docker-compose - All Dockerfiles updated - Tests pass - docker-compose.flows.yml: yaml.safe_load() succeeds with correct var names

task.title: Fix deploy_flow() — make config parameter optional with from_env() default
task.description: deploy_flow() has a required DeployConfig positional parameter with no default. This makes the flow uncallable from Prefect UI and untestable without building config. Add DeployConfig.from_env() and make the parameter optional.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_deploy_flow_config.py: - test_deploy_config_from_env_reads_tracking_uri: set MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI env var; DeployConfig.from_env() must return config with that tracking_uri - test_deploy_config_from_env_reads_champion_dir: set CHECKPOINT_DIR; verify - test_deploy_flow_callable_without_config: call deploy_flow.__wrapped__() with no config; verify it doesn't raise TypeError (uses from_env default) - test_deploy_flow_accepts_explicit_config: pass DeployConfig explicitly; verify used
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update src/minivess/orchestration/deploy_flow.py: - Add DeployConfig.from_env() classmethod that reads from environment - Change signature: def deploy_flow(config: DeployConfig | None = None, ...) - At function start: if config is None: config = DeployConfig.from_env() Update src/minivess/config/deploy_config.py: add from_env() classmethod
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - deploy_flow() callable with no arguments (uses env vars) - Explicit DeployConfig still works - All unit tests pass - mypy: clean

task.title: Fix silent exception swallowing — replace sentinel strings with None, fix MLflow failures
task.description: Multiple flows use "no_upstream" sentinel strings and catch all exceptions silently. This violates CLAUDE.md "Never Silent-Fail" rule. Fix upstream discovery to return None, and MLflow logging failures to raise RuntimeError with actionable instructions. The find_upstream_safely() helper from T-02 provides the safe upstream discovery; this task ensures callers handle None correctly.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_no_sentinels.py: - test_no_sentinel_strings_in_flows: use ast.parse() + ast.walk() to scan all flow files for string literal "no_upstream"; assert not found - test_upstream_returns_none_on_failure: mock FlowContract.find_upstream_run to raise; find_upstream_safely() must return None (not raise, not return sentinel) - test_mlflow_failure_logs_warning: mock mlflow.start_run() to raise; verify warning logged (we allow graceful degradation for MLflow — it's observability, not blocking) Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_upstream_none_handling.py: - test_training_flow_handles_none_upstream: call training_flow with mock that returns None for upstream; verify flow proceeds and upstream_data_run_id is None in result
tdd_spec.green_phase: Update all flows that use "no_upstream" sentinel: - Replace: upstream_data_run_id: str = "no_upstream" With: upstream_data_run_id: str | None = None - Update all downstream consumers that compared to "no_upstream" to check for None - Ensure TrainingFlowResult, PostTrainingFlowResult etc. use str | None for upstream fields - Verify find_upstream_safely() (T-02) handles exceptions and returns None
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - grep for "no_upstream" returns 0 results (use Grep tool, not regex) - All None-handling tests pass - mypy: str | None types properly annotated

task.title: Fix bare exception handlers — ONNX export failure must propagate
task.description: In deploy_flow.py and post_training_flow.py, critical operations (ONNX export, champion discovery) are caught by bare except and execution continues. The flow returns success even when ONNX export failed. This silently corrupts the deployment.

tdd_spec.green_phase: Update deploy_flow.py: - ONNX export: remove try-except; let exception propagate (it's blocking) - Champion discovery: same - Add failed_operations: list[str] to DeployFlowResult Update post_training_flow.py: - SWA: blocking — re-raise on failure - Calibration/conformal: best-effort — catch, log, add to failed list in result Update PostTrainingFlowResult (T-13): add failed_operations: list[str] field
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - ONNX export failure causes deploy_flow to raise - Optional plugin failures populate failed_operations but don't fail the flow - Tests pass

task.title: Add preflight env var validation to training_flow and analysis_flow
task.description: Critical paths (SPLITS_DIR, CHECKPOINT_DIR) are read from environment without validation. When missing, flow fails cryptically mid-execution. Add preflight validation that raises RuntimeError with clear setup instructions.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_preflight_validation.py: - test_training_flow_raises_on_missing_splits_dir: unset SPLITS_DIR; call training_flow.__wrapped__() without splits_dir; assert RuntimeError raised with message containing "SPLITS_DIR" - test_training_flow_raises_on_missing_checkpoint_dir: unset CHECKPOINT_DIR; same - test_analysis_flow_raises_on_missing_output_dir: unset ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR; same - test_validation_passes_when_vars_set: set all required vars; no RuntimeError
tdd_spec.green_phase: Add _validate_required_env() helpers in each affected flow: training_flow: validate SPLITS_DIR, CHECKPOINT_DIR analysis_flow: validate ANALYSIS_OUTPUT_DIR (after T-08 rename) Call at the start of each flow function before any heavy computation. Error message format: "Required env var {var} not set. Set it with:\n export {var}=/path/to/dir"
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - RuntimeError raised with clear message when required env vars missing - No RuntimeError when vars are set - Tests pass - mypy: clean

task.title: Add PostTrainingFlowResult dataclass to match pattern of other flow results
task.description: post_training_flow() returns dict[str, Any] — untyped and inconsistent with TrainingFlowResult and DeployFlowResult. Create a typed dataclass.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_post_training_result.py: - test_result_is_dataclass: import PostTrainingFlowResult; assert dataclasses.is_dataclass() - test_result_has_required_fields: verify fields: flow_name, status, mlflow_run_id, upstream_training_run_id, swa_completed, calibration_completed, conformal_completed, failed_operations - test_result_default_values: PostTrainingFlowResult() has sensible defaults (not raises) - test_post_training_flow_returns_typed_result: mock all heavy operations; verify post_training_flow.__wrapped__() returns PostTrainingFlowResult (not dict)
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Add to src/minivess/orchestration/flows/post_training_flow.py (at top): @dataclass class PostTrainingFlowResult: flow_name: str = "post-training-flow" status: str = "completed" mlflow_run_id: str | None = None upstream_training_run_id: str | None = None swa_completed: bool = False calibration_completed: bool = False conformal_completed: bool = False failed_operations: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
Update post_training_flow() return type annotation. Update all return statements to return PostTrainingFlowResult instances.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - PostTrainingFlowResult is a proper dataclass - post_training_flow() has return type annotation -> PostTrainingFlowResult - mypy: clean - Tests pass

task.title: Add explicit upstream_run_id params to all flows for deterministic chaining
task.description: Flows only discover upstream runs via MLflow tag queries (FlowContract), which is fragile when tags are missing. Add optional upstream_run_id parameters to all consumer flows so explicit chaining is possible (for CI, trigger chains, tests). Closes #412 (analysis_flow specifically) and #418 (all flows generally).

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/unit/orchestration/test_explicit_upstream.py: - test_training_flow_accepts_upstream_data_run_id: verify training_flow signature has upstream_data_run_id: str | None = None parameter - test_post_training_flow_accepts_upstream_training_run_id: same - test_analysis_flow_accepts_upstream_training_run_id: same - test_deploy_flow_accepts_upstream_analysis_run_id: same - test_dashboard_flow_accepts_upstream_analysis_run_id: same - test_explicit_id_skips_mlflow_discovery: mock FlowContract; when explicit ID provided, find_upstream_run() must NOT be called (mock.assert_not_called()) - test_none_id_triggers_mlflow_discovery: when parameter is None, find_upstream_run() IS called Write tests/integration/orchestration/test_trigger_chain.py: - test_trigger_chain_passes_run_ids_explicitly: simulate PipelineTriggerChain; verify each downstream flow receives the upstream run ID explicitly
tdd_spec.green_phase:
Update flow signatures (where not already done): - post_training_flow(..., upstream_training_run_id: str | None = None) - run_analysis_flow(..., upstream_training_run_id: str | None = None) - deploy_flow(..., upstream_analysis_run_id: str | None = None) - dashboard_flow(..., upstream_analysis_run_id: str | None = None)
Update each flow body: if upstream_X_run_id is not None: discovered_upstream = {"run_id": upstream_X_run_id} else: discovered_upstream = find_upstream_safely(...)
Update PipelineTriggerChain in orchestration/trigger.py to pass run IDs explicitly.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All 5 consumer flows have optional upstream run ID parameter - Explicit ID skips MLflow discovery (verified by mock assertion) - PipelineTriggerChain uses explicit IDs - All tests pass, mypy clean

task.title: End-to-end integration test: flow chain with all fixes applied
task.description: After all 14 preceding tasks, verify the complete flow chain works end-to-end with mocked MLflow and Prefect. This is the acceptance test for issue #404.

tdd_spec.red_phase: Write tests/integration/orchestration/test_full_flow_chain.py: - test_full_chain_with_mocked_mlflow: mock mlflow, mock all heavy computation; run PipelineTriggerChain from data → train → post_training → analyze → deploy; verify each flow: (a) logs with correct FLOW_NAME_* constant (b) receives the upstream run ID explicitly (not via discovery) (c) returns typed result dataclass (not dict) (d) no "no_upstream" sentinel strings in any result - test_all_flow_names_in_constants: for each @flow in all flow files, verify the name is one of the FLOW_NAME_* constants (AST + constants lookup) - test_all_experiment_names_in_constants: same for MLflow experiment names - test_docker_compose_env_vars_consistent: load docker-compose.flows.yml with yaml.safe_load(); verify all OUTPUT env vars end with _OUTPUT_DIR
tdd_spec.green_phase: Fix any remaining issues found by the integration tests. Ensure PipelineTriggerChain passes all explicit run IDs through the chain.
tdd_spec.acceptance_criteria: - All integration tests pass - Full flow chain runs with zero sentinel strings - All constant references verified - ruff + mypy: clean across all orchestration modules - Issue #404 closed

wave.description: Complete DRY chain and fix tag mismatches
